name: Kailua Farmers' Market
tagline: Thursday evening market run by the same folks behind KCC — 20 years of Hawaii-grown-only produce, mochi, and street corn.
parking: Free parking at Kailua Town Center
hours: Thursdays 4pm–7pm sharp. Alarm sounds at 7pm and all sales stop immediately
bestTime: Arrive before 5pm — popular items like Mochi Aulelei Hawaii taro mochi and Nozawa Farms street corn sell out early
talkStory: Run by the Hawaii Farm Bureau, the same organization behind the famous KCC Farmers Market. This one's been going for 20 years and strictly sells Hawaii-grown products — produce, flowers, beef, pork, seafood, and ready-to-eat foods. Look for the family-run German bakery and handmade salsas. Kids love the chalk and live music. The 7pm alarm is no joke — when it goes off, everything shuts down, so don't arrive at 6:45 expecting to browse. Located at 609 Kailua Rd in Kailua Town Center.
